summary: Punchy guitars, strong second set, Good Lovin' climax
blurb: Shows a punchy guitar tone and solid rhythm work, with Loser, Easy Wind and Good Lovin' as highlights. The second set shines on China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and Bird Song, closing with an energetic Good Lovin'. Overall performance is strong despite a few weaker moments like Ripple in the first set.
review:
On February 21, 1971 at the Capitol Theater in Port Chester, the Dead were deep into their early‑70s evolution. Jerry’s guitar was described as gorgeous and punchy, especially on the driving leads of Cumberland Blues and Truckin', while Bob Weir took center stage with tight rhythm on Loser and Easy Wind. Phil held down a steady bass groove, even though one reviewer noted a spinning cord during the night. Pigpen rescued a lyrical slip in Easy Wind and added his characteristic organ/harmonica flavor throughout.

The first set opened strong with Cold Rain And Snow and built momentum through Loser, Easy Wind and a lively Playing In The Band, but dipped a bit on Ripple and Truckin' according to some ears. The second set was the real high‑energy zone: China Cat Sunflower seamlessly segued into I Know You Rider, Bird Song floated beautifully, and the band closed with a double Good Lovin' that lifted the entire concert. Bill Kreutzmann’s “slamming” drumming carried the show as the sole drummer—Mickey Hart was still absent—but his absence didn’t hurt the flow. With clear soundboard captures available (especially the Cantor‑Crouch SBD), this performance stands out as a solid snapshot of the band’s transition period.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings praise the performance (positive sentiment on guitar tone, rhythm work, second‑set energy) with only one mixed review and no consistent negative criticism, justifying a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Clear, crisp soundboard capture that highlights the standout Loser, Good Lovin' and Easy Wind performances with minimal technical flaws.

key_highlights: Punchy guitar tones and solid rhythm work throughout the night.
Second‑set energy peaks with China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider and a double Good Lovin'.
Pigpen’s lyrical rescue on Easy Wind adds a memorable touch.
Consistently good soundboard recordings; Cantor‑Crouch SBD is especially clear.

band_performance.Jerry: Guitar shines with gorgeous, punchy tones and strong leads on Cumberland Blues and Truckin', adding charisma throughout the show.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation; a minor cable spin was noted but did not affect his groove.
band_performance.Bob: Front‑and‑center rhythm work on Loser and Easy Wind, cues the audience howl before the second set, and maintains tight comping all night.
band_performance.Brent: Ron "Pigpen" McKernan supplies organ and harmonica, notably rescuing the Easy Wind lyrics and adding soulful texture to the mix.
band_performance.Mickey: Absent – this was the first show without Mickey; his spot is covered by Bill alone.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann delivers slamming, sole‑drummer drumming that keeps tight time and drives the energy forward.
